findings.title: NC-4/NC-5 assertion queries run under superuser pool (brain), not SET ROLE brain_app — diminished RLS verification signal for those two assertions
findings.note: The assertions confirm correct data (0 active sessions, status=suspended) which are functional facts on app_user/user_session; app_user has RLS disabled (0002_auth.sql:42 — DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY confirmed by inspection); user_session RLS is scoped by app.current_user_id not org (0002_auth.sql:82-84). The RLS-isolation assertions (NC-1/NC-2/NC-3) correctly use SET LOCAL ROLE brain_app. NC-4/NC-5 functional correctness is sound; the test-quality gap is that SET ROLE brain_app is not asserted for those specific assertion queries. Not a runtime data-leak risk. Deferrable to MED backlog.

findings.title: resolveMembership helper in member.routes.ts accepts rawPgPool as optional — null fallback is unreachable in prod but silently degrades the route-layer defence-in-depth lookup on misconfiguration
findings.note: main.ts:313 passes rawPgPool unconditionally so the fallback path is unreachable in production. If rawPgPool is ever nil (misconfiguration), the null return causes a 404 rather than letting the service-level D-9 org assertion be the sole guard — which is itself still correct. Deferrable LOW.
